title: atriplex hastata subsp. polonica
intro: Atriplex hastata subsp. polonica, also known as the Polish orach, is a subspecies of the orach plant that is native to Europe and is characterized by its shrub-like growth habit and small, green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Atriplex hastata subsp. polonica have been shown to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, which can be used to treat various conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
